methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $36M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

data.narrative:
Living Carbon, a Series A biotech company founded by Maddie Hall, has raised $36M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private—operating in the same Biotech & Health sector and at same or adjacent funding stages.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-Progression anchors to the $36M raised and applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next round, implying a $72M valuation. Peer-Median analysis examines 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Eikon Therapeutics at $3.3B, Xaira at $2.7B, Neko Health at $1.8B, New Limit and Asimov each near $800M, plus two others), yielding a median valuation of $810M and a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-Comp anchoring is not applicable; the sector's public biotech multiples (P/S median of 6.1x) cannot be applied without Living Carbon's disclosed revenue.

The triangulated range is $61M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $441M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around stage classification within the peer set and the significant valuation spread among early-stage biotech comparables. Near-term clarity on revenue trajectory and technical validation would narrow this range materially.
